title: delphinium peregrinum subsp. halteratum
intro: Delphinium peregrinum subsp. halteratum, commonly known as the 'Halterated Larkspur', is a plant species belonging to the Delphinium genus and is native to certain parts of the world. It has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly as a cardiac stimulant and for its expectorant and antispasmodic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as delphineine. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antibacterial properties. The herbal preparations of Delphinium peregrinum subsp. halteratum typically involve drying the plant material and using it in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
